$comment: US-S05-07: Static mapping check_id → explanation (article, penalty, deadline, business_impact)

passport-presence.business_impact: Agent Passport is the core identity document for your AI system. Without it, you cannot demonstrate compliance to regulators, and deployers cannot verify the system's risk classification or intended use.

passport-completeness.business_impact: An incomplete passport means gaps in your compliance record. Regulators require full documentation of AI system capabilities, limitations, and risk management measures.

ai-disclosure.business_impact: Users must be informed they are interacting with AI. Failure to disclose AI involvement erodes trust and violates transparency obligations that are already enforceable.

content-marking.business_impact: AI-generated content must be machine-detectable. Without marking (C2PA, watermarks, metadata), downstream systems cannot verify content provenance.

interaction-logging.business_impact: Structured logging of AI interactions is required for audit trails, incident investigation, and demonstrating human oversight. Without logs, you cannot prove compliant operation.

ai-literacy.business_impact: All personnel working with AI must have sufficient AI literacy. Undocumented training programs expose your organization to liability if operators misuse AI systems.

gpai-transparency.business_impact: General-purpose AI models require model cards documenting capabilities, limitations, and training data. This enables downstream providers to assess risks.

compliance-metadata.business_impact: Machine-readable compliance metadata enables automated verification. Without it, integration partners and regulators cannot programmatically assess your compliance status.

documentation.business_impact: Technical documentation is a foundational requirement. It must be maintained throughout the AI system lifecycle and made available to competent authorities upon request.

l2-ai-literacy.business_impact: Your AI literacy document exists but lacks required sections. Incomplete training documentation fails the minimum standard for demonstrating organizational AI competence.

l2-art5-screening.business_impact: Art. 5 prohibited practices screening is the highest-penalty obligation. Your screening document must demonstrate systematic assessment of all 8 prohibited categories.

l2-tech-documentation.business_impact: Technical documentation must include system architecture, data sources, training methodology, and performance metrics. Incomplete docs block conformity assessment.

l2-monitoring-policy.business_impact: Monitoring policy must define scope, frequency, metrics, and escalation procedures. Without it, you cannot demonstrate ongoing post-market surveillance.

l2-worker-notification.business_impact: Workers and their representatives must be notified before AI deployment affects them. Missing notification is a direct violation of worker protection obligations.

l2-fria.business_impact: Fundamental Rights Impact Assessment is mandatory for high-risk AI in public sector and certain private uses. It must analyze impacts on health, safety, and fundamental rights.

l2-declaration-conformity.business_impact: The EU Declaration of Conformity is a legally binding statement. Without it, your AI system cannot be placed on the EU market or put into service.

l2-incident-report.business_impact: Incident reporting is required for serious incidents. A template must exist before incidents occur to ensure timely reporting within the mandatory timeframe.

l3-missing-bias-testing.business_impact: Bias testing tools are absent from your dependencies. Without them, you cannot demonstrate that your AI system has been tested for discriminatory outcomes across protected characteristics.

l3-log-retention.business_impact: Log retention configuration is missing. AI system logs must be retained for a period appropriate to the system's risk level and purpose, typically 6-12 months minimum.

l3-env-config.business_impact: Environment configuration lacks compliance-relevant settings. Proper configuration ensures logging, monitoring, and safety features are consistently enabled across deployments.

l3-ci-compliance.business_impact: CI/CD pipeline lacks compliance checks. Automated compliance gates prevent non-compliant code from reaching production, reducing risk of regulatory violations.

l3-banned-package.business_impact: A banned or high-risk dependency was detected. Using packages with known security vulnerabilities or prohibited licenses undermines your system's cybersecurity and legal standing.

l4-bare-llm.business_impact: Direct LLM API calls without compliance wrapper bypass all safety hooks: prohibited content detection, PII sanitization, bias checking, and disclosure verification. Every unwrapped call is a potential violation.

l4-disclosure.business_impact: AI disclosure patterns found — this is positive. Ensure disclosure components are visible in all user-facing interfaces where AI is involved.

l4-human-oversight.business_impact: Human oversight mechanisms enable operators to monitor, intervene, and override AI decisions. Without them, you cannot meet the human-in-the-loop requirement for high-risk systems.

l4-kill-switch.business_impact: A kill switch or feature flag allows immediate shutdown of AI functionality. This is essential for incident response and required for human oversight of high-risk systems.

l4-content-marking.business_impact: Content marking code patterns detected. Verify that AI-generated content is marked in a machine-readable format (C2PA, IPTC, custom metadata) across all output channels.

l4-logging.business_impact: Structured logging patterns are present in your codebase. Ensure logs capture: input/output hashes, model version, latency, token usage, and compliance check results.

l4-data-governance.business_impact: Data governance patterns control data quality, lineage, and consent. Without them, you cannot demonstrate that training and input data meet EU AI Act standards.

l4-record-keeping.business_impact: Record-keeping enables traceability throughout the AI system lifecycle. Audit trails must capture compliance events, configuration changes, and incident responses.

l4-accuracy-robustness.business_impact: Accuracy and robustness validation ensures your AI system performs reliably. Without testing, you cannot demonstrate the system meets performance benchmarks required by Art. 15.

l4-cybersecurity.business_impact: Cybersecurity measures (rate limiting, input sanitization, access control) protect against adversarial attacks and data manipulation. Essential for system resilience.

l4-deployer-monitoring.business_impact: Deployer monitoring patterns track model drift, performance degradation, and anomalies. Required for post-market surveillance and incident detection.

l4-gpai-transparency.business_impact: GPAI transparency code patterns document model capabilities. Ensure model cards, training data documentation, and capability descriptions are complete and accessible.

l4-conformity-assessment.business_impact: Conformity assessment patterns support the formal evaluation process. High-risk AI systems must undergo conformity assessment before market placement.

l4-security-risk.business_impact: Unsafe code patterns (eval, pickle, command injection) create exploitable vulnerabilities. These undermine your system's cybersecurity posture required by Art. 15(4).

behavioral-constraints.business_impact: Behavioral constraints define how your AI agent escalates decisions and handles PII. High-risk agents without structured escalation rules lack the human oversight required by Art. 14. Missing data boundaries indicate gaps in the risk management system required by Art. 9.

cross-doc-code-mismatch.business_impact: Your documentation claims capabilities that are not implemented in code (or vice versa). Regulators will verify documentation against actual system behavior — mismatches indicate governance failures.

cross-sdk-no-disclosure.business_impact: AI SDK is used in your project but no disclosure is shown to users. This is a direct Art. 50(1) violation — users interacting with your AI must be explicitly informed.

cross-logging-no-retention.business_impact: Logging exists but without retention policy. Logs without defined retention can be deleted prematurely (losing audit trail) or retained too long (GDPR violation).

cross-kill-switch-no-test.business_impact: Kill switch exists but has no tests. An untested kill switch may fail during an incident, leaving you unable to shut down a malfunctioning AI system when it matters most.

undeclared-permission.business_impact: Tool permissions used in code but not declared in Agent Passport. Undeclared permissions indicate governance gaps — regulators require full transparency about AI agent capabilities.

unused-declared-permission.business_impact: Passport declares tool permissions not found in code. Stale declarations reduce trust in passport accuracy and may indicate outdated documentation.

cross-permission-passport-mismatch.business_impact: Undeclared permissions combined with unwrapped LLM calls indicate a compounding governance failure. The AI system operates outside its declared boundaries without compliance safeguards.
